info.title: Marketplace Reporting API
info.description:
**For detailed information on how best to implement the Reporting API Suite, refer to the [Reporting Integration Guide](/eats/guides/reporting).**

This endpoint will allow a developer to request standardized batch reports (e.g. Payment Details Report) to be generated by Uber Eats. Once the report has been generated, a `eats.report.success` webhook notification will be sent to the URL configured in [the app dashboard](https://developer.uber.com//dashboard). This webhook notification will contain URL(s) you can use for downloading each section of the report.

**NOTE: Transaction reporting can be large and can take some time in generation, so upon initial report API request, a workflow_id will be returned as an acknowledgement in response.**

# Table of contents
- [Create Report](https://developer.uber.com/docs/eats/references/api/reporting_suite#operation/postCreateReport)
- [Report Created Webhook Event Type](https://developer.uber.com/docs/eats/references/api/reporting_suite#/paths/report_created/post)

# Request Constraints
**Time Range Constraints**

There are constraints on the time range / lookback period for which the reports can be requested. These constraints are designed to optimize performance and data accuracy. For e.g. the Payment Details Report can be requested for a maximum range of 32 days in a single request and the Order History Report can be requested only for the [T - 188, T - 2] time period (in days) where T represents the current date.

| Report Type                            | Lookback Period        | Range Period (Days) |
|:---------------------------------------|:----------------------:|:-------------------:|
| Payment Details Report                 | N/A                    | 30                  |
| Orders And Items Report                | N/A                    | 15                  |
| Finance Summary Report                 | N/A                    | 30                  |
| Order Errors Menu Item Report          | [T - 188, T - 2]       | N/A                 |
| Order Errors Transaction Report        | [T - 190, T - 4]       | N/A                 |
| Order History Report                   | [T - 188, T - 2]       | N/A                 |
| Downtime Report                        | [T - 188, T - 2]       | N/A                 |
| Customer and Delivery Feedback Report  | [T - 188, T - 2]       | N/A                 |
| Menu Item Feedback Report              | [T - 188, T - 2]       | N/A                 |
| Billing Details Report                 | [T - 1825, T - 2]      | N/A                 |

requestBody.description: Request body for creating a report

workflow_id.description: A reference ID used to identify this report generation job in subsequent API calls or webhook notifications. There will be a future API that takes this `workflow_id` as argument and returns the job status/data.

403.description: Forbidden - User unauthorized to view data for this restaurant or store.

413.description: Payload Too Large - The generated report is too large limit. Try requesting a smaller date-range.

414.description: Precondition Failed - There was an input validation issue, e.g. start-date is after the end-date.

429.description: Rate Limited - Exceeded rate limit. Please wait before trying again.

post.description:
This webhook is sent to notify you once a report job (generated via [POST /eats/report](https://developer.uber.com/docs/eats/references/api/reporting_suite#operation/postCreateReport)) has completed.  You can access the report data via the `report_metadata.sections[]` fields.

Your service should return an HTTP 200 response code with an empty response body to acknowledge receipt of the webhook event. If Uber does not receive a 200 acknowledgement response, the webhook event will be resent based on an exponential backoff algorithm (i.e. starting at 1 second after the initial attempt, then 2 seconds, then 4 seconds etc.) until 7 total events were sent without a response.

- `eats.report.success` - notification a report was created successfully.

### Webhook Guide
Check [Webhook Guide](https://developer.uber.com/docs/eats/guides/webhooks).

eats.report.success.description: Webhook request object for report success

event_type.description: The type of the event, e.g. `eats.report.success`. Always check this field as new event types may be added in the future without notice.

event_id.description: Unique identifier for the webhook notifications. It’s possible that the same webhook notification might be sent multiple times if believe the first attempt failed.

job_id.description: Unique identifier for consuming a report generation response.

report_type.description: This will match the report type in your initial POST request and is provided for easier reference. See above for possible values.

start_time_ms.description: The time that the report generation job started processing.

end_time_ms.description: The time that the report generation job finished processing.

section_id.description: Some reports are split into separate sections (or files).

content_type.description: MIME-type of the report section, e.g. `text/csv`.

create_report_request_object.description: Request object for creating a report

store_uuids.description: Uber Eats Store UUID(s) against which the report should be generated. One way to enumerate these is by calling the [GET /stores](https://developer.uber.com/docs/eats/references/api/store_suite#tag/GetStores) endpoint.

group_uuids.description: Uber Eats Group UUID(s) against which the report should be generated. This can be used to fetch reports of all stores under the Group

start_date.description:
The start date from which this report should contain data. This is store’s local timezone.

Note: `PAYMENT_DETAILS_REPORT` also supports `YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ss` format.

end_date.description:
The end date up to which this report should contain data. This is store’s local timezone.

Note: `PAYMENT_DETAILS_REPORT` also supports `YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ss` format.

report_type.description: 10 type of reports are supported by this API.

auth.description: This API uses OAuth 2 with the implicit grant flow. [More info](https://developer.uber.com/docs/eats/guides/authentication)

scopes.eats.report: Indicates a token has permission to create a report.
